af_score_components.notes: IMPORTANT: Volcano Agent SDK is a TypeScript SDK for BUILDING AI agents, not an MCP server itself. It consumes MCP tools. No MCP server. API keys for each LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Bedrock, etc.) — no unified credential store. HTTP-accessible MCP endpoints required (local stdio MCP servers need wrapping). Multi-agent delegation loops possible with poor prompt design. auth_complexity=70 for multiple provider API keys.

security_score_components.security_notes: All LLM provider API calls use HTTPS. Multiple API key management is operator responsibility. Kong-backed open-source project with security awareness. TypeScript type safety reduces certain vulnerability classes. OpenTelemetry tracing for security observability. No compliance certifications as SDK framework. Secrets stored in environment variables.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SDK reliability depends on upstream LLM providers and MCP tool servers. Built-in retry guidance. Multi-provider support provides fallback options. Parallel execution and streaming supported. OpenTelemetry integration for observability. v1.1.0 shows stable development. Kong backing provides credibility. No SLA as open-source SDK.

af_score_components.notes: Vonage/Nexmo communications API. No MCP server. Two different auth systems depending on API: SMS uses API key + secret, Voice and others use JWT. This split auth is confusing for agents. Legacy Nexmo and modern Vonage APIs coexist with different base URLs. OpenAPI spec available. auth_complexity=45 for dual auth requirement (API key for some, JWT for others).

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. JWT for Voice API provides strong token-based auth. API key + secret for SMS is functional. SOC2/GDPR compliant. Webhook signature verification available. Dual auth systems increase configuration complexity. OpenAPI spec available for integration. Split auth could lead to misconfiguration if agents use wrong method.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Vonage has status page. SOC2 certified. Legacy Nexmo/modern Vonage API coexistence creates fragmentation. No idempotency support. Offset pagination. International number availability varies. Dashboard less intuitive than Twilio for debugging. Retrofit guidance from Nexmo to Vonage APIs available but adds confusion.

af_score_components.notes: Vultr affordable cloud infrastructure API. No MCP server. Single API key with full account access (no fine-grained scoping). OpenAPI spec available. Instance creation returns immediately but polling required until 'active'. VKE Kubernetes clusters take 5-10 minutes. Rate limits undocumented. auth_complexity=85 for simple single API key (but treat as highest privilege).

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. Single API key with full account access is a security concern — no fine-grained scoping. SOC2 certified. Key must be stored in secrets manager (full account access means compromise = full infrastructure compromise). No scope limitations to restrict what agent can do. Recommend using separate Vultr accounts for different agents/environments.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Vultr has status page and SLA. SOC2 certified. Instance creation async — polling required. VKE cluster provisioning takes 5-10 minutes. Rate limits undocumented (implement exponential backoff on 429). Cursor pagination. Region and plan IDs use short codes requiring mapping. Reliable infrastructure for VPS/Kubernetes workloads.

af_score_components.notes: Weights & Biases ML experiment tracking. No MCP server. API key via WANDB_API_KEY env var. Both REST and GraphQL APIs (most programmatic access via GraphQL, not REST). Service accounts for CI/CD. Run IDs auto-generated (set explicitly for reproducibility). Sweep agents must have termination conditions. Weave (LLM monitoring) uses separate SDK calls. auth_complexity=90 for simple env-var API key.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. API key via env var or service accounts for automation. SOC2/GDPR compliant. No fine-grained permission scoping per key (user-scoped). Artifact deletion can break downstream lineage (operational security). Service accounts recommended for CI/CD over personal API keys. No credentials in URLs.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: W&B has status page. SOC2 certified. Cursor pagination. Retry guidance documented. Artifact versioned DAG provides lineage integrity. Sweep termination conditions required for reliability. Both REST and GraphQL APIs maintained. Weave product adds LLM monitoring layer. Enterprise tier with SLA available.

af_score_components.notes: Wasabi S3-compatible object storage. No MCP server. AWS SigV4 auth — any S3 SDK works by changing endpoint URL. Access key + secret key identical to S3 model. 90-day minimum retention policy (deletions before 90 days still charged). 4KB minimum file size billing. $6.99/month minimum. No native CDN. auth_complexity=80 for SigV4 (familiar to S3 users, non-trivial for others).

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. AWS SigV4 signing provides strong authentication. SOC2/ISO27001/HIPAA/GDPR compliant. Access key + secret key model (same as S3) with IAM-compatible scoping. Bucket policies for fine-grained access control. Encryption at rest. Bucket region selection permanent (cannot move). Strong compliance posture for storage.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2/ISO27001 certified with status page. S3-compatible — any S3 retry logic applies. Full idempotency for storage operations. Cursor pagination. 90-day retention policy affects delete-heavy workloads. No webhooks (polling only). Bucket region permanent affects disaster recovery planning. Enterprise reliability for object storage.

af_score_components.notes: Wassette is a WebAssembly sandbox runtime for MCP tools (Microsoft). Has MCP quality 68. No auth required (OCI registry follows standard container auth). WebAssembly Components must be compiled/published to OCI registry before use. Not production-ready (early development). Wasmtime browser-grade isolation. Component model toolchain adds developer complexity. auth_complexity=90 as no auth needed.

security_score_components.security_notes: WebAssembly sandboxing via Wasmtime provides strong isolation — the core security value proposition. Sandboxed tools cannot access host filesystem or network beyond declared capabilities. Microsoft-backed project with security focus. OCI registry follows standard auth patterns. Early stage — security architecture is sound but not production-validated. Existing security 85.0 reflects strong sandbox design.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Not production-ready — explicitly early-stage. WebAssembly component compilation required. OCI registry dependency for component distribution. Cold-start latency for loading from registry. No SLA. Microsoft backing provides long-term development confidence but current maturity is limited. WebAssembly runtime (Wasmtime) is stable; the MCP wrapper layer is newer.

af_score_components.notes: Wave Accounting GraphQL API. No MCP server. OAuth2 required (access tokens expire in 60min, refresh tokens available). Documentation sparse and outdated post-H&R Block acquisition. GraphQL means agents must know Wave's schema. Rate limits undocumented. Business ID required in most mutations. API development has slowed since acquisition. auth_complexity=60 for OAuth2 with token expiry management.

security_score_components.security_notes: All HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with scoped access (account:read, account:write, business:read, business:write). Access tokens expire in 60 minutes (good security practice). SOC2 certified. Bearer token via Authorization header. H&R Block ownership provides enterprise security infrastructure. No credentials in URLs.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2 certified with H&R Block infrastructure. API development slowed since acquisition — new features rare. Documentation outdated increases integration risk. Rate limits undocumented. No idempotency support. OAuth token 60-minute expiry requires refresh handling. Webhooks available. Suitable for simple freelancer accounting automation but not for growing businesses.

af_score_components.notes: Wax on-device RAG for iOS/macOS with MCP. Has MCP quality 65. No auth (fully local). Swift 6.2+/Xcode 16.0+ required. Apple Silicon recommended (Intel fallback with higher latency). MCP server via npx waxmcp (Node.js required). Foundation Models integration requires iOS 26+. Single portable .wax file format. auth_complexity=100 for no auth (local only).

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication — fully local, no external API calls. All embeddings and retrieval on-device (strong privacy). Metal GPU processing stays on device. No compliance certifications. Swift implementation with Apple platform security. Portable .wax file should be protected from unauthorized access. Existing security 80.0 reflects strong privacy-first design.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local library — reliability depends on Apple platform. Swift 6.2+ requirement limits compatibility. Intel Mac fallback to CPU adds latency variance. v0.1.8 — early stage. Metal GPU acceleration for Apple Silicon. Full idempotency for retrieval operations. Node.js dependency for MCP wrapper adds external dependency. No SLA as community project.

af_score_components.notes: WCGW provides AI agents with full shell and file editing access on local machine via MCP. Has MCP quality 80. No auth (runs with current user permissions). Three modes: architect (read-only), code-writer (edit), wcgw (full shell). Requires files be read before editing. Windows only via WSL. CRITICAL: grants unrestricted shell access to agent. auth_complexity=100 for no auth (local user permissions).

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL SECURITY CONSIDERATION: Unrestricted shell access means agent can delete files, run network commands, access any file the user can access, install software, and exfiltrate data. No authentication or authorization. Three operational modes provide some control (architect = read-only) but relying on AI agent self-restraint is not a security boundary. Only use in trusted environments with full understanding of risks. Existing security 45.0 reflects this risk.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool at v5.6.1 — mature version number indicates stability. Chunked pagination for large outputs. Three operational modes give some workflow structure. File read-before-edit protection reduces accidental overwrites. WSL-only on Windows limits platform support. Active development with good documentation. No SLA as open-source tool.

af_score_components.notes: WeatherAPI weather data service. No MCP server. API key as query parameter (security concern but simple). Generous free tier (3-day forecast). 14-day forecast requires paid plan. Historical data 1 year on free, more on paid. Air quality and astronomy endpoints count toward quota. Location parameter accepts multiple formats (city, lat/lon, zip, IP). auth_complexity=85 for simple API key despite URL exposure.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced but API key in query parameter is a security concern — keys appear in server logs and referrer headers. No fine-grained scopes. GDPR compliant. Single API key with broad access. Low-sensitivity data (weather is public) mitigates security impact of key exposure. Mitigation: use server-side proxy to hide key from clients. secret_handling=58 due to key-in-URL pattern.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: WeatherAPI has status page. API versioned and stable. Full idempotency for weather lookups. No pagination required (single request per query). Good error messages. Free tier monthly quota resets — agents must monitor usage. 14-day forecast on paid tier. Historical data limits by plan. Reliable for weather integration use cases.
